title: Why Does the Moon?
poem_text:
Why does the moon wear silver shoes?
And where do all the lost socks choose?
Do shadows giggle when they play?
Or hide and never run away?
Can clouds remember how to cry?
Or do they just forget and fly?
Why do the stars all wink at me?
And what do fireflies agree?
Does time get tired of ticking true?
Or does it dream in colors new?
If I could ask the wind one thing...
What would the wind ask me to sing?
cover_context: A child lying on grass at dusk, staring up as silver shoes dangle from a crescent moon against a twilight sky.
style_prompt: Children's musical poem, guitar and gentle beatbox, 108 BPM, dreamy and wondering, spacious, leaves room to think, clear warm vocal, every word easy to understand
